title: Business Insider: GlobalTech Lays Off 1,200 Workers in Major Cost-Cutting Move Amid Revenue Decline
snippet: GlobalTech Corp lays off 1,200 employees as revenue falls 12% and the company scrambles to cut costs.
content: GlobalTech Corp has laid off 1,200 employees in what analysts are calling the latest sign of the tech industry's ongoing correction. The mass layoff, announced this week, affects workers across the company's Cloud Services and Enterprise Software divisions — historically the core of GlobalTech's business. The restructuring comes as the company struggles with a steep 12% year-over-year decline in revenue and mounting competitive pressure from Microsoft and Amazon in the cloud space. Employees affected by the involuntary terminations are being offered minimal severance packages as the company tightens its belt. Industry analyst Mark Peterson of Gartner noted: 'Companies like GlobalTech that expanded rapidly during COVID are now rationalizing their headcount to protect margins.' GTC shares fell 2.1% on the news.
